subject: First Aid & Safety
title: Hurricane Anatomy
description: A quick, practical guide to Hurricane Anatomy.

hook.text: A hurricane is a heat engine powered by warm ocean water—releasing energy equal to 10,000 nuclear bombs daily.

buildup.text: Warm ocean water above 80°F evaporates, rises, and creates a low-pressure spiral system.

discovery.text: The eye—calm, clear, and warm—is surrounded by the eyewall with the most violent winds.

twist.text: Storm surge—not wind—causes 49% of hurricane deaths. Water rises up to 20 feet above normal.

climax.text: Climate change is warming oceans, increasing the proportion of the most intense hurricanes.

punchline.text: Warm water fuels the engine. Storm surge does the killing. Respect both.

quiz.question: What causes the most hurricane-related deaths?
